title: Jennifer Runyon Dies: ‘Ghostbusters’ And ‘A Very Brady Christmas’ Actress Was 65
full_text: Skip to main content Dismiss News Banner Breaking News Writers Guild Awards Winners: 'Sinners' & 'One Battle After Another' Take Screenplay Prizes; 'The Pitt' Goes 3-For-3 Read the full story By Natalie Oganesyan Natalie Oganesyan Weekend Editor @nat_oganesyan More Stories By Natalile Stephen Colbert Accepts WGA East’s Walter Bernstein Award: “The Revolution … Was Going To Be Televised, And Then Paramount Bought It” Elijah Wood Plays Coy About Return As Frodo, But Says He Wouldn’t Want Anyone Else Portraying The Character “As Long As I’m Alive And Able” Jennifer Runyon Dies: ‘Ghostbusters’ And ‘A Very Brady Christmas’ Actress Was 65 View All March 8, 2026 3:27pm (L-R): Steven Tash, Jennifer Runyon, Bill Murray in 'Ghostbusters' Columbia Pictures / courtesy Everett Collection Jennifer Runyon , an actress best known for her work in Ghostbusters and A Very Brady Christmas , has died after a brief battle with cancer, according to her friend, fellow actress and Bewitched star Erin Murphy. She was 65. “So sad to share that my friend Jennifer Runyon Corman has passed away after a brief battle with cancer. 💔 Some people you just know you’ll be friends with before you even meet. She was a special lady. I’ll miss you Jenn. My thoughts are with your family and beautiful children,” Murphy wrote on Instagram. View this post on Instagram Corman first began appearing on TV and in film in 1980, logging credits in series like The Fall Guy, Boone and The Master, as well as booking a recurring role in the long-running NBC soap Another World . In 1984, she appeared opposite Tim Matheson in comedy Up the Creek. That same year, she also featured in the iconic supernatural comedy starring Bill Murray, portraying a female student undergoing a paranormal assessment. Related Stories News Dan Aykroyd Considers His 'Casper' Cameo Part Of 'Ghostbusters' Canon: "No Doubt" News 'Ghostbusters' Animated Movie In The Works From Sony Animation And Netflix In the years following, she also appeared in shows like Magnum, P.I.; Quantum Leap ; Murder, She Wrote and Beverly Hills, 90210 . Runyon also played Gwendolyn Pierce in the first season of family sitcom Charles in Charge , the object of Scott Baio’s titular character’s affection. Watch on Deadline Runyon also took over Susan Olsen’s role as the youngest Brady child, Cindy, in the 1988 made-for-TV movie A Very Brady Christmas , which reunites the family around the holiday. Born April 1, 1960, in Chicago, Runyon married Todd Corman — a basketball coach, assistant director and nephew to actor Roger Corman — in 1991. The couple had two children, a son, Wyatt, and a daughter, Bayley. Also an actress who’s logged episodes of 9-1-1, Chilling Adventures of Sabrina and Jane the Virgin , Bayley Corman posted a tribute to her mom , writing: “all of the best parts of me came from you. i would give anything for one more day together.” ‘One Battle After Another’ Takes Top Prize At Cinematographers’ ASC Awards Pixar Originals Rebound With ‘Hoppers’ $88M WW; Why ‘The Bride!’ Bombed Colbert Lobs One More Jab At Paramount As He Accepts Guild Honor ‘Sinners’, ‘Frankenstein’ & ‘Zootopia 2’ Top MPSE Winners Read More About: 1 Comments

title: receiving this year’s Walter Bernstein Award
full_text: Skip to main content Dismiss News Banner Breaking News Writers Guild Awards Winners: 'Sinners' & 'One Battle After Another' Take Screenplay Prizes; 'The Pitt' Goes 3-For-3 Read the full story By Katie Campione Katie Campione Senior TV & Labor Reporter @katie_campione More Stories By Katherine WGA East President Tom Fontana Talks AMPTP Negotiations & Paramount-WBD Merger At WGA Awards: “Democracy Is At Stake” L.A. Writers Shrug Off WGA West Award Ceremony Cancellation From Staff Union Picket Line, Urging Leadership To “Treat Our Staff Members With Respect” SAG-AFTRA & Studios Extend Negotiations Into Next Week Amid Progress As Start Of WGA Talks Nears View All January 26, 2026 10:42am Stephen Colbert Scott Kowalchyk/CBS With The Late Show set to end in May , Stephen Colbert is receiving this year’s Walter Bernstein Award from the Writers Guild of America East. The honor will be bestowed on March 8 at the WGA Awards ‘ East Coast ceremony in New York, which takes place concurrently with the WGA’s West Coast ceremony in Los Angeles. Related Stories News ‘Blossom’ & ‘The Ranch’ Creator Don Reo Set For WGA West’s 2026 Paddy Chayefsky Award Below The Line 2026 Awards Season Calendar: Dates For Oscars, Guilds, Grammys, Tonys & More The Bernstein Award, which was established in 2017, is presented to a member “who has demonstrated with creativity, grace and bravery a willingness to confront social injustice in the face of adversity.” It is not an annual honor and has only been bestowed twice before to Jelani Cobb and Ron Nyswaner. In his statement, Colbert said “It’s a great honor to receive the Walter Bernstein award from our Guild, and I assume this is mostly for my work on The Dana Carvey Show (possibly available on Blu-Ray!)” Watch on Deadline Jokes aside, WGAE President Tom Fontana makes it clear in his own statement that Colbert is receiving the honor for the same reason he got a standing ovation at the Emmys in September — his bullishness on Donald Trump and, particularly, CBS parent company Paramount Global’s $16 million settlement with the POTUS over the way that CBS News ‘ 60 Minutes edited an interview with Kamala Harris. “Walter would be proud to see the award go to Colbert,” Fontana said in a statement. “Colbert put his career on the line to fight back against a threat to democracy that’s much greater than just the loss of the beloved Late Show . Stephen Colbert has built an empire by speaking truth to power, and we’re beyond proud to have him as a WGAE member.” CBS has said The Late Show cancellation was “purely a financial decision against a challenging backdrop in late night.” Colbert, says he’s been told that the network is planning to get out of late-night altogether because it’s not profitable anymore, but the host has repeatedly pointed out that his show was axed two days after he called Paramount Global’s settlement of Trump’s lawsuit a “big fat bribe”. Colbert has served as host, writer and executive producer on the Emmy and Peabody Award-winning The Late Show with Stephen Colbert since 2015. Earlier in his career he was a correspondent for The Daily Show with Jon Stewart from 1997 to 2005 and the host of The Colbert Report from 2005 to 2014.
